steps.title: Calculate the confidence interval
steps.content:
The margin of error is $0.02\text{ oz}$ around the estimate of $27.8\text{ oz}$:
$$27.8 - 0.02 = 27.78\text{ oz (lower bound)}$$$$27.8 + 0.02 = 27.82\text{ oz (upper bound)}$$

steps.title: Interpret what the interval represents
steps.content:
Margin of error applies to the estimate of the population mean, not to individual weights (choice B) or the known sample data (choice C).

Choice D ignores the margin of error. The correct interpretation is that we estimate the population mean falls within $[27.78, 27.82]\text{ oz}$.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
